page_data.title: Healthy Habits
page_data.learning_objective: Understand and discuss basic healthy habits.

activity.instruction: One clear choice or discussion task for “Healthy Habits” using 3–6 pictures. Add one short sentence starter.
activity.evidence: A one-word choice or short supported sentence.
activity.blank_space: Use choice circles or one sentence starter.

teacher_prompt.facilitation: Name the choices and ask the child to select one.
questions: Name the choices and ask the child to select one. Then ask: “What can you tell me about healthy habits?”
teacher_prompt.expected_response: A one-word choice or short supported sentence.
teacher_prompt.scaffold: Point or gesture; offer two choices; model one word or phrase; invite repetition.

parent_prompt.conversation: Invite the child to revisit “Healthy Habits” using the same model language and accept pointing, gesture, a one-word answer, or the approved short phrase.
parent_prompt.home_activity: Repeat the primary activity once with familiar home objects or family conversation; stop after one successful response and affirm the child's effort.

illustration.focal_point: One dominant learning focus for “Healthy Habits”.
illustration.style: Premium preschool workbook; friendly 2D vector-like forms; clean white base; soft pastel accents; thick rounded outlines.
negative_constraints: Do not redraw, imitate, replace, distort, crop, or generate the BCube logo; reserve the specified header position for the official vector asset.
Do not add unapproved text, labels, activities, frames, balloons, answer spaces, decorations, watermarks, QR codes, or third-party marks.
Do not exceed two core child activities plus one optional extension; retain the page-specific lower limit when it is stricter.
Do not use dark full-page backgrounds, gradients that impair legibility, visual clutter, tiny objects, thin faint outlines, or unsafe edge placement.
Do not introduce frightening imagery, stereotypes, shame, competitive ranking, inaccessible instructions, or developmentally inappropriate writing load.
Do not alter Star's approved identity, colours, proportions, costume, expression language, or role.
Do not leave unexplained blank space: every box, line, card, balloon, frame, and open area must serve the named learning action.
Do not output mockups, perspective views, device screens, page curls, hands holding the page, crop marks inside trim, or multiple page variants.
No frightening wrong options, shame, trick questions, or unrelated tasks.
illustration.illustrator_notes: Gold-standard interior prompt. Individual execution locked: 2 activities; Use choice circles or one sentence starter.
illustration.approved_source_blueprint: INDIVIDUAL PAGE EXECUTION BLUEPRINT — PAGE 17: Learning objective: Understand and discuss basic healthy habits. Primary child activity: One clear choice or discussion task for “Healthy Habits” using 3–6 pictures. Secondary activity: Add one short sentence starter. Teacher/parent prompt: Name the choices and ask the child to select one. Expected child response: A one-word choice or short supported sentence. Blank-space allocation: Use choice circles or one sentence starter. Activity load: 2 activities. Prohibited extras: No frightening wrong options, shame, trick questions, or unrelated tasks. Every box, balloon, line, card, and frame must have a stated learning purpose. Do not invent additional activities or unexplained blank areas.

compiled_prompt:
# BCube Production Prompt v3 — CC-NUR-V3-P017

## 1. Release metadata

- Prompt ID: CC-NUR-V3-P017
- Prompt package version: 3.0.0
- Compiler: BCube Prompt Compiler 3.0.0
- Release date: 2026-07-15
- Book: Communication Champions — Gold Edition
- Level: Nursery (3+)
- Module: Module 1 – Me & My Family
- Page: 17 of 41
- Exact page title: Healthy Habits
- Source status: FINAL
- Source workbook SHA-256: caecd24a409b4f531b6018b2076d42cc9222af2f198e7c9e5835cde0bc54bfba

## 2. Production command

Create exactly one final, flat, front-facing A4 portrait workbook page for page 17, “Healthy Habits”. Treat this package as a deterministic production specification for an illustrator, layout artist, or image-generation workflow. Preserve all approved page-specific instructions. Do not invent content. Produce only this page; do not produce explanations or alternates.

## 3. Compiler order and precedence

- 1. Publishing Engine
- 2. Design Engine
- 3. Visual Grammar Engine
- 4. Educational Engine
- 5. Teaching Engine
- 6. Parent Partnership Engine
- 7. Illustration Engine
- 8. Character Engine
- 9. Quality Assurance and Prompt Compiler

Apply rules in this precedence: approved page-specific blueprint > approved unit/book rule > global rule. An override is valid only when explicitly stated in this package. Resolve no conflict by guessing; stop and flag it for editorial review.

## 4. Publishing Engine

- Format: A4 portrait, 210 × 297 mm.
- Production resolution: 300 DPI, print-ready; CMYK-safe colour decisions; 3 mm bleed; keep essential content at least 10 mm inside trim.
- Header/title: Use a 20–28 mm header zone. Reserve top-left placement for the official BCube Future Academy vector logo and centre the exact page title “Healthy Habits”. Never generate or redraw the logo.
- Footer: Use a 10–14 mm footer zone and show page number 17 only at bottom right; no extra footer copy.
- Cover-only age badge rule: Use Nursery (3+) on the cover only; do not repeat it on interior pages.
- Output: one print-ready page with clean trim, bleed, safe-zone discipline, and editable text/logo layers in final assembly.

## 5. Design Engine

- Page type: Sequencing.
- Activity/layout requirement: 2-by-2 habit scenes.
- Activity load: 2 activities.
- Blank-space allocation: Use choice circles or one sentence starter.
- Use a clear top-to-bottom hierarchy: identity/title, one dominant learning focus, child action area, compact adult support when required, then footer.
- Background and palette: Clean white with soft pastel accents; no dark full-page backgrounds; Soft peach, mint, lavender, sky blue, sunshine yellow; limited bright accents.
- Typography and marks: large, highly legible early-years type; thick rounded outlines; generous spacing; no essential information carried by colour alone.

## 6. Visual Grammar Engine

- Establish one unmistakable focal point directly serving “Healthy Habits”.
- Guide the eye from title to model/example to child action to supported response.
- Use repetition only to clarify sequence, comparison, choice, or turn-taking.
- Keep cognitive load appropriate for age 3+: large targets, few choices, clear grouping, obvious action affordances, and no decorative competition with the task.
- Every visual element must support the learning objective or production identity.

## 7. Educational Engine

- Learning objective: Understand and discuss basic healthy habits.
- Primary child activity: One clear choice or discussion task for “Healthy Habits” using 3–6 pictures.
- Secondary speaking/reflection: Add one short sentence starter.
- Observable evidence: A one-word choice or short supported sentence.
- Use inclusive, familiar, concrete contexts. Accept speech, pointing, gesture, imitation, drawing, colouring, matching, or adult-supported dictation exactly where the blueprint permits.

## 8. Teaching Engine

- Facilitation: Name the choices and ask the child to select one.
- Model first, invite one child turn, pause for processing, then affirm the attempt without correcting through shame.
- Approved question: Name the choices and ask the child to select one. Then ask: “What can you tell me about healthy habits?”
- Expected response: A one-word choice or short supported sentence.
- Scaffold in this order: point/gesture → offer two choices → model one word or phrase → invite repetition. Do not force a longer response.

## 9. Parent Partnership Engine

- Conversation prompt: Invite the child to revisit “Healthy Habits” using the same model language and accept pointing, gesture, a one-word answer, or the approved short phrase.
- Home reinforcement: Repeat the primary activity once with familiar home objects or family conversation; stop after one successful response and affirm the child's effort.
- Use common household materials only; no purchase, printing, device, or specialist knowledge is required.
- The parent prompt supports the page objective and must not create a new scored activity.

## 10. Illustration Engine

- Rendering style: premium preschool workbook illustration; friendly 2D vector-like forms; clean white base; soft pastel accents; thick, clean, rounded black outlines; large enclosed colouring areas where applicable.
- Scene/layout anchor: 2-by-2 habit scenes.
- Page-specific composition: Build one coherent sequencing composition for “Healthy Habits”. Visually demonstrate: One clear choice or discussion task for “Healthy Habits” using 3–6 pictures. Support it with: Add one short sentence starter. Preserve the response evidence: A one-word choice or short supported sentence.
- Illustrator notes: Gold-standard interior prompt. Individual execution locked: 2 activities; Use choice circles or one sentence starter.
- Ensure natural expressions, correct anatomy, age-appropriate proportions, inclusive representation, clear object separation, and unobstructed response spaces.
- Keep all visible copy exact, editable, correctly spelled, and inside safe zones.

## 11. Character Engine

- Star mascot use: Required.
- Use only the locked Star identity: bright yellow rounded five-point star body; warm expressive face; blue shoes; blue cape when the approved pose requires it; friendly preschool proportions; consistent scale, colours, and facial language.
- Star demonstrates, encourages, points, listens, or celebrates without completing the child's answer space.
- Children and adults must show respectful interaction, culturally inclusive presentation, natural pose logic, and clear conversational turn-taking.

## 12. Approved page-specific Gold execution blueprint

INDIVIDUAL PAGE EXECUTION BLUEPRINT — PAGE 17: Learning objective: Understand and discuss basic healthy habits. Primary child activity: One clear choice or discussion task for “Healthy Habits” using 3–6 pictures. Secondary activity: Add one short sentence starter. Teacher/parent prompt: Name the choices and ask the child to select one. Expected child response: A one-word choice or short supported sentence. Blank-space allocation: Use choice circles or one sentence starter. Activity load: 2 activities. Prohibited extras: No frightening wrong options, shame, trick questions, or unrelated tasks. Every box, balloon, line, card, and frame must have a stated learning purpose. Do not invent additional activities or unexplained blank areas.

## 13. Negative constraints

- Do not redraw, imitate, replace, distort, crop, or generate the BCube logo; reserve the specified header position for the official vector asset.
- Do not add unapproved text, labels, activities, frames, balloons, answer spaces, decorations, watermarks, QR codes, or third-party marks.
- Do not exceed two core child activities plus one optional extension; retain the page-specific lower limit when it is stricter.
- Do not use dark full-page backgrounds, gradients that impair legibility, visual clutter, tiny objects, thin faint outlines, or unsafe edge placement.
- Do not introduce frightening imagery, stereotypes, shame, competitive ranking, inaccessible instructions, or developmentally inappropriate writing load.
- Do not alter Star's approved identity, colours, proportions, costume, expression language, or role.
- Do not leave unexplained blank space: every box, line, card, balloon, frame, and open area must serve the named learning action.
- Do not output mockups, perspective views, device screens, page curls, hands holding the page, crop marks inside trim, or multiple page variants.
- No frightening wrong options, shame, trick questions, or unrelated tasks.

## 14. Gold QA acceptance gate

Before release, verify: exact page identity; A4 geometry; 3 mm bleed; 10 mm safe margin; official logo reservation; exact title; interior page number where applicable; one learning objective; blueprint-matched activity load; meaningful blank space; teacher/parent facilitation; expected response; mascot continuity; inclusive character quality; print suitability; no prohibited extras; no unresolved variables. Any critical failure blocks release.

## 15. Compiler footer

Prompt ID CC-NUR-V3-P017 | v3.0.0 | source page 17/41 | precedence page > unit/book > global | Gold gate 100/100 | critical defects 0 | status GOLD CERTIFIED.
